questions.question: Explain the concept of a friend function in C++ and state its advantages.
keyPoints: Friend function is a non-member function with access to private members
Declared using 'friend' keyword inside the class
Advantages: allows operator overloading, maintains encapsulation while providing flexibility
Enables stream operator overloading for user-defined types

questions.question: What are the rules regarding friendship in C++? Explain with examples.
keyPoints: Friendship is not transitive - A friend of B is not friend of C
Friendship is not inherited - derived classes don't inherit friendship
Friendship is not reciprocal - A is friend of B doesn't mean B is friend of A
A function can be friend to multiple classes

questions.question: Differentiate between a member function and a friend function.
keyPoints: Member function belongs to class; friend function does not
Member function called using object; friend function called like normal function
Member function has 'this' pointer; friend function does not
Friend function declared with 'friend' keyword inside class

questions.question: Write a C++ program to overload the + operator using a friend function.
keyPoints: Class definition with private data members
Friend function declaration inside class
Operator overloading function definition
Main function demonstrating addition of two objects

questions.question: What is a friend class? When and why is it used?
keyPoints: Friend class - all member functions of one class are friends of another
Used when two classes need to share private data
Provides more access than single friend function
Example: Iterator class accessing container's private elements

questions.question: Explain the difference between << operator when overloaded as member function vs friend function.
keyPoints: Member function: obj << cout (unnatural syntax)
Friend function: cout << obj (natural syntax)
Friend function takes both operands as parameters
Member function uses implicit 'this' for left operand

questions.question: Write a C++ program to display private data of a class using a friend function.
keyPoints: Class with private data members
Friend function declaration
Friend function accessing private data directly
Main function creating object and calling friend function

questions.question: Write a complete C++ program to implement a class 'Complex' with real and imaginary parts. Overload operators +, -, and << using friend functions to perform addition, subtraction and display complex numbers.
keyPoints: Complex class with private real and imaginary members
Friend function declarations for + and - operators and <<
Overloaded + returns Complex object with summed values
Overloaded - returns Complex object with subtracted values
Overloaded << operator to display in a+bi format
Main function demonstrating all operations

questions.question: Explain the concept of friendship in C++ with respect to encapsulation. Discuss its advantages and disadvantages, and when should one use friend functions instead of member functions.
keyPoints: Friendship breaks data hiding but provides controlled access
Advantages: operator overloading flexibility, stream operators, tightly coupled classes
Disadvantages: violates encapsulation, increases coupling, less maintainable
Use friend when: operator overloading, stream operators, two classes closely related
Prefer member functions when: operation belongs naturally to class

questions.question: Create a C++ program with two classes: 'Box' and 'Warehouse'. Declare 'Warehouse' as a friend class of 'Box'. Write appropriate member functions to demonstrate how Warehouse can access Box's private members.
keyPoints: Box class with private length, width, height
Warehouse class declared as friend of Box
Warehouse member functions accessing Box's private data
Demonstration in main with Box and Warehouse objects
Shows direct access without getter/setter

questions.question: Write a C++ program to demonstrate that friendship is neither transitive nor inherited with appropriate examples and output.
keyPoints: Code showing A friend of B, B friend of C doesn't make A friend of C
Code showing derived friend class doesn't get friendship
Class structures demonstrating both scenarios
Output proving friendship is not transitive/inherited
